items.text:
Green Commute Rewards Program

Starting September 12, the Office of Sustainability is launching the Green Commute Rewards program to encourage sustainable transportation. Participants who commute by bicycle, on foot, or via public transit can earn points redeemable for campus gift cards and merchandise. Each logged commute earns 10 points; 500 points are required for a $25 gift card.

Program rules:
- Register your commute through the online portal by September 5.
- Log each trip within 24 hours using the mobile app.
- Only commutes between 6:00 AM and 10:00 PM on weekdays qualify.
- Maximum of two commutes per day.
- A $5 penalty applies for any false logging.

Points can be redeemed at the Campus Services Office in Anderson Hall, Room 204, from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. For questions, contact sustainability@university.edu.

Join us in reducing campus carbon emissions!

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must participants do to earn points?

options.A: To commute between 6:00 AM and 10:00 PM on weekends
options.B: To log each trip within 24 hours using the mobile app
options.C: To redeem points at the Campus Services Office in Anderson Hall
options.D: To commute by bicycle, on foot, or via private vehicle

questions.explanation: The notice states: 'Log each trip within 24 hours using the mobile app.'

questions.stem: What is the penalty for false logging in the program?

options.A: A penalty of $10 is applied for each false log
options.B: A penalty of $25 is deducted from earned points
options.C: A penalty of $5 is applied for each false log
options.D: A penalty of 500 points is deducted from the account

questions.explanation: The notice states: 'A $5 penalty applies for any false logging.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Green Commute Rewards program?

options.A: It is designed to reduce carbon emissions by encouraging sustainable commuting
options.B: It requires participants to purchase a $25 gift card to start earning points
options.C: It allows participants to log commutes at any time of day or night
options.D: It provides a $5 bonus for each commute logged within 24 hours

questions.explanation: The notice ends with 'Join us in reducing campus carbon emissions,' implying the program's environmental goal.

items.text:
BIOL 101: Introduction to Biology – Lab Component Grading

Instructor: Dr. Sarah Chen. Office: Science Building, Room 310.

The lab component of BIOL 101 constitutes 35% of the total course grade. Grading is as follows:
- Lab Reports (40% of lab grade): Six reports due throughout the semester.
- Final Lab Practical (30% of lab grade): Administered on December 10, from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM.
- Participation and Notebook (30% of lab grade): Assessed weekly.

Key Policies:
- Late lab reports lose 10% per day; no reports accepted after 72 hours.
- Attendance is mandatory; one unexcused absence results in a 5% deduction from the participation grade.
- The final lab practical covers material from weeks 6–14.
- A passing grade in the lab component (60% or higher) is required to pass the course.

Important Dates:
- September 15: First lab report due.
- November 20: Last day to drop the lab without a grade penalty.
- December 10: Final Lab Practical.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this syllabus excerpt?

options.A: To describe the schedule for lab reports and the final practical
options.B: To explain the grading breakdown and policies for the lab component
options.C: To list the penalties for late submissions and unexcused absences
options.D: To inform students about the instructor's office hours and location

questions.explanation: The excerpt focuses on the lab grading (35% of course grade), its components, and key policies.

questions.stem: According to the syllabus, what happens if a student submits a lab report two days late?

options.A: The report is not accepted after 72 hours of the deadline
options.B: A 10% deduction is applied for each day of lateness
options.C: The report loses 20% of its possible points
options.D: The student receives a 5% deduction from participation grade

questions.explanation: The policy states: 'Late lab reports lose 10% per day; no reports accepted after 72 hours.' Two days late means 20% deduction.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the final lab practical?

options.A: It is administered on the last day of the semester
options.B: It covers topics from the second half of the semester only
options.C: It is worth more than the lab reports in the overall lab grade
options.D: It requires a score of 60% to pass the lab component overall

questions.explanation: The syllabus says the final covers 'weeks 6–14,' implying the first half (weeks 1–5) is not tested.

items.text:
ArtByLena

Just finished animating my latest stop-motion short film! It's called "Paper Petals" and it took me three months to complete. 🎬✨ I used over 2,000 photographs, each one a tiny adjustment of paper flowers and leaves. The most frustrating part was keeping the lighting consistent – one cloud passing by could ruin an entire scene! But the result is totally worth it. 🎥

I'm planning a small screening on campus next Friday at 7:00 PM in the Student Union Theater. Admission is free, but there will be a donation jar for the Art Department's supplies fund. I'd love to see your faces there!

What's the most tedious art project you've ever worked on? Share your stories below! #stopmotion #paperart #studentfilm #indieanimation

questions.stem: According to the post, what was the most challenging part of creating "Paper Petals"?

options.A: Keeping the lighting consistent throughout the shots
options.B: Adjusting over 2,000 paper flowers and leaves
options.C: Finding a theater on campus for the screening
options.D: Animating the film in only three months

questions.explanation: The post says: 'The most frustrating part was keeping the lighting consistent – one cloud passing by could ruin an entire scene!'

questions.stem: The word 'tedious' in the last paragraph is closest in meaning to:
questions.explanation: In context, 'tedious' refers to repetitive, time-consuming work, synonymous with 'slow and boring.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the screening on campus?

options.A: It is part of a larger art exhibition at the Student Union
options.B: It is free to attend, but donations for supplies are encouraged
options.C: It requires advance tickets purchased at the Art Department
options.D: It will feature multiple student films, including "Paper Petals"

questions.explanation: The post states 'Admission is free, but there will be a donation jar for the Art Department's supplies fund,' implying free entry with optional donations.
